hooks.prompt:
A new git commit was just created in this repository. Decide whether README.md needs to be updated and, if so, update it.

Steps:
1. Run `git log -1 --stat` to see the latest commit's message and changed files.
2. If the commit is documentation-only, test-only, refactor, chore, style, or a fix that does not change user-visible behavior, do nothing and exit. Do NOT touch README.md.
3. If the commit adds a new feature, new checker, new CLI flag, new section coverage, or otherwise changes user-visible behavior, read README.md and update only the sections that are now stale (Features list, Checkers table, MISRA Coverage table, Roadmap, version/test counts). Keep edits surgical and consistent with existing English/中文 bilingual structure.
4. If you updated README.md, stage and amend it into the just-created commit with `git commit --amend --no-edit -- README.md`. Do not create a new commit. Do not push.
5. Report in one short sentence what you did (or that no update was needed).
